ideas.one_liner: Precision-only throughput and exponential emulation correction.

sections.abandonment_condition: Precision-only variant does not improve MAPE on the FP8/FP4 subset.
sections.falsification_path: Run the precision-only variant on held-out validation across BF16, FP16, FP8, and FP4; reject this candidate if the FP8/FP4 subset does not improve.
hidden_assumptions: Per-precision Tensor Core and MUFU throughput constants explain a meaningful share of FA4 runtime error.
FP8/FP4 runtime error is not already captured by bytes and FLOPs in the baseline roofline.
A precision-only term is separable enough to test without the occupancy and bandwidth terms.
sections.hypothesis: A precision-only throughput and exponential emulation correction will improve held-out MAPE on the FP8/FP4 subset relative to the accepted FA4 paper roofline baseline.
sections.local_optimum_risk: Medium - this is the cheapest ablation of C1 and may miss occupancy or memory hierarchy effects.
sections.minimal_experiment: Implement only the precision-specific MMA/MUFU correction; calibrate constants on the calibration split; compare held-out MAPE on each precision subset against the baseline and C1 ablations.
sections.one_sentence_claim: Adding precision-specific Tensor Core and exponential throughput terms should reduce FP8/FP4 prediction error without changing the rest of the roofline model.
sections.outside_family_alternative: C3 tests occupancy-only correction; C1 combines precision with occupancy and TMA/L2 bandwidth.
sections.strongest_rejection_case: Precision is already represented well enough by FLOP count and bytes, so the missing runtime error comes from occupancy, memory movement, or scheduling.
sections.targeted_bottleneck: The FA4 roofline treats precision only through FLOP count.
sections.why_now: This is the cheapest first ablation of C1 and directly tests whether precision is the dominant missing term.

summary: Pre-idea draft for serious alternative C2.
title: Pre-Idea Draft: Precision-Only Throughput Correction
